content.problemStatement:
Write a program that uses a for-each loop to print the square of each element in list of NNN space separated integers, but skips elements greater than 101010.

Check the sample input and output below for further clarity.

**Note**: Output the square of each element on a new line.
constraints: All squares fit into a signed 64-bit integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of integers; the second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the square of every integer that is less than or equal to 101010, each on its own line, preserving the order of appearance. No output is produced for numbers greater than 101010.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All numbers are ≤ 101010, so their squares (2², 10², 12², 6², 15²) are printed in order.
Every number satisfies the condition, therefore 5², 20² and 30² are output.
All numbers are small; their squares are printed.

solution.approach: The task is straightforward: iterate over each number of every test case, check the condition x <= 101010 and, if true, output x*x. A single linear scan is sufficient because each element is processed independently. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
